principles: The phase and level structure is product-owner approved and immutable for this milestone.
Level 0 is fully authored for absolute beginners.
Levels 1-39 are subject-specific planned outlines, not fake completion.
Every command placement is read from curated curriculum-command-placement.json.

phases.title: ABSOLUTE BEGINNER
phases.purpose: Give a true beginner language, confidence, and mental models before vendor syntax.
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored Level 0 and preview planned levels honestly.

phases.title: SWITCHING FOUNDATIONS
phases.purpose: Build switching, VLAN, trunk, STP, and aggregation foundations.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: ROUTING AND NETWORK SERVICES
phases.purpose: Connect VLANs, routes, and essential services into reachable networks.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: WIRELESS AND VOICE
phases.purpose: Explain wireless and real-time traffic support at the access edge.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: SECURITY
phases.purpose: Introduce secure management, switch-port protection, and access control.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: RESILIENCE AND ENTERPRISE SWITCHING
phases.purpose: Cover availability, stacking, virtual chassis, and enterprise topology.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: MONITORING AND TROUBLESHOOTING
phases.purpose: Teach evidence collection, troubleshooting method, and endpoint diagnostics.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: REAL OPERATIONAL WORK
phases.purpose: Prepare learners for controlled changes, deployment, recovery, and communication.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: HOSPITALITY AND BUSINESS SCENARIOS
phases.purpose: Ground network support in guest, staff, and business-service scenarios.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

phases.title: ADVANCED NETWORKING
phases.purpose: Preview advanced switching, routing, multicast, QoS, automation, and operations.
phases.target_student: networking learner building toward technician readiness
phases.completion_rule: Complete authored lessons, practice, verification, rollback, and review evidence when available.

levels.title: Welcome to Networking
levels.plain_language_summary: A welcoming introduction to networks, technician thinking, simulation boundaries, and safe learning.
levels.why_it_matters: Beginners need a safe mental model before command syntax.
learning_outcomes: Explain LAN, WAN and the internet.
Explain What a network technician does.

modules.title: What is a network?
modules.purpose: Teach: What is a network?
objectives: Explain What is a network? in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: What is a network?
lessons.objective: Identify a network as devices connected to share information.
lessons.mission: A guest laptop reaches a booking page because several devices pass information along.
lessons.learn: Two or more devices connected so they can exchange useful information.
lessons.see: Guest laptop -> wall port -> switch -> gateway -> booking service.

predict.question: Which phrase best describes a network?
predict.explanation: The important idea is communication between connected devices.

tryInteraction.prompt: Choose the picture that shows devices sharing a service.
tryInteraction.explanation: The important idea is communication between connected devices.

lessons.explain: The important idea is communication between connected devices.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can explain what a network is in one sentence.
lessons.real_life_example: A guest laptop reaches a booking page because several devices pass information along.
lessons.visual_text_model: Guest laptop -> wall port -> switch -> gateway -> booking service.

modules.title: Why devices communicate
modules.purpose: Teach: Why devices communicate
objectives: Explain Why devices communicate in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: Why devices communicate
lessons.objective: Explain why endpoints, services, and network devices exchange data.
lessons.mission: A POS terminal sends a payment request and receives an approval response.
lessons.learn: Devices communicate to request, deliver, confirm, or update information.
lessons.see: POS terminal asks -> payment service answers -> receipt prints.

predict.question: Why does a client send data to a server?
answer_choices: To request or update a service
predict.explanation: Communication has a purpose: a request, response, or update.

tryInteraction.prompt: Pick the request and response in the example.
answer_choices: To request or update a service
tryInteraction.explanation: Communication has a purpose: a request, response, or update.

lessons.explain: Communication has a purpose: a request, response, or update.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can name a reason devices talk.
lessons.continue: Continue to LAN, WAN and internet.
lessons.real_life_example: A POS terminal sends a payment request and receives an approval response.
lessons.visual_text_model: POS terminal asks -> payment service answers -> receipt prints.

modules.title: LAN, WAN and the internet
modules.purpose: Teach: LAN, WAN and the internet
objectives: Explain LAN, WAN and the internet in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: LAN, WAN and the internet
lessons.objective: Distinguish local networks, wide-area links, and internet reachability.
lessons.mission: A resort office printer is local, while a cloud booking platform is reached through the internet.
lessons.learn: A LAN is local to a room, floor, or site. A WAN links sites. The internet links to public services.
lessons.see: Room LAN -> hotel network -> ISP/WAN -> internet service.

predict.question: Which problem is most likely local?
answer_choices: A vendor cloud region is down
predict.explanation: Scope matters: local failures and external failures need different evidence.

tryInteraction.prompt: Sort three examples into LAN, WAN, and internet.
answer_choices: A vendor cloud region is down
tryInteraction.explanation: Scope matters: local failures and external failures need different evidence.

lessons.explain: Scope matters: local failures and external failures need different evidence.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can separate local and remote network scope.
lessons.real_life_example: A resort office printer is local, while a cloud booking platform is reached through the internet.
lessons.visual_text_model: Room LAN -> hotel network -> ISP/WAN -> internet service.

modules.title: What a network technician does
modules.purpose: Teach: What a network technician does
objectives: Explain What a network technician does in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: What a network technician does
lessons.objective: Describe the technician role as evidence-first support and safe change.
lessons.mission: A CCTV camera is offline; the technician checks link state before changing VLANs.
lessons.learn: A technician defines the problem, gathers evidence, changes only with approval, verifies, and documents.
lessons.see: Symptom -> evidence -> safe action -> verification -> note.

predict.question: What should come before a configuration change?
predict.explanation: Safe technicians use evidence before action.

tryInteraction.explanation: Safe technicians use evidence before action.

lessons.explain: Safe technicians use evidence before action.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can describe the technician workflow.
lessons.real_life_example: A CCTV camera is offline; the technician checks link state before changing VLANs.
lessons.visual_text_model: Symptom -> evidence -> safe action -> verification -> note.

modules.title: Real devices versus Command Doctor simulation
modules.purpose: Teach: Real devices versus Command Doctor simulation
objectives: Explain Real devices versus Command Doctor simulation in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: Real devices versus Command Doctor simulation
lessons.objective: Separate local simulation from production devices.
lessons.mission: A simulated switch can teach command order without touching a guest-room switch.
lessons.learn: Command Doctor is local training. Production devices are real systems with guest and business impact.
lessons.see: Simulation sandbox || production network; the boundary must stay clear.

predict.question: Where should a first attempt happen?
predict.explanation: Practice belongs in simulation until approved production work exists.

tryInteraction.prompt: Choose whether examples are simulated or production-impacting.
tryInteraction.explanation: Practice belongs in simulation until approved production work exists.

lessons.explain: Practice belongs in simulation until approved production work exists.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can explain the simulation boundary.
lessons.real_life_example: A simulated switch can teach command order without touching a guest-room switch.
lessons.visual_text_model: Simulation sandbox || production network; the boundary must stay clear.

modules.title: Safe learning rules
modules.purpose: Teach: Safe learning rules
objectives: Explain Safe learning rules in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: Safe learning rules
lessons.objective: Apply beginner rules that prevent unsafe changes.
lessons.mission: A read-only command is safe evidence; saving a wrong config can preserve an outage.
lessons.learn: Start read-only, avoid unsupported changes, verify before save, and know rollback before action.
lessons.see: Read-only evidence -> approved change -> verify -> save or rollback.

predict.question: Which action is safest first?
predict.explanation: Safe order protects users and services.

tryInteraction.prompt: Choose the safe first action in three mini cases.
tryInteraction.explanation: Safe order protects users and services.

lessons.explain: Safe order protects users and services.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can state the beginner safety order.
lessons.real_life_example: A read-only command is safe evidence; saving a wrong config can preserve an outage.
lessons.visual_text_model: Read-only evidence -> approved change -> verify -> save or rollback.

modules.title: Beginner glossary
modules.purpose: Teach: Beginner glossary
objectives: Explain Beginner glossary in beginner language.
Keep production devices separate from local practice.

lessons.title: Beginner glossary
lessons.objective: Define first-week networking words in plain language.
lessons.mission: A phone, AP, and printer are hosts connected through switch ports and VLANs.
lessons.learn: Plain words make command output readable: hosts use links, switches connect local devices, gateways reach other networks.
lessons.see: Host -> switch port -> VLAN -> gateway -> other network.

predict.question: What is a gateway used for?
predict.explanation: Glossary words turn output into a story.

tryInteraction.prompt: Match each word to the simplest definition.
tryInteraction.explanation: Glossary words turn output into a story.

lessons.explain: Glossary words turn output into a story.
lessons.confidence_prompt: You can define the core beginner terms.
lessons.real_life_example: A phone, AP, and printer are hosts connected through switch ports and VLANs.
lessons.visual_text_model: Host -> switch port -> VLAN -> gateway -> other network.

modules.title: Level checkpoint
modules.purpose: Teach: Level checkpoint
objectives: Explain Level checkpoint in beginner language.

lessons.title: Level checkpoint
lessons.mission: Before command syntax, a learner proves they understand safe evidence, scope, and simulation.
lessons.learn: The checkpoint confirms the beginner can explain networks, scope, technician behavior, and safety boundaries.
lessons.see: Question -> answer -> explanation -> confidence -> orientation complete.

predict.question: What must be true before Level 0 completes?
predict.explanation: Level 0 completion records orientation only, not command mastery.

tryInteraction.explanation: Level 0 completion records orientation only, not command mastery.

lessons.explain: Level 0 completion records orientation only, not command mastery.
